name: The 7 Sacraments

phase_0.boundary_condition: The Church as the mystical body of Christ — the hermetic vessel containing all seven sacraments as a unified economy of salvation.

VINIT.justification: The void state before any sacrament is received; the soul in its uninitialized, pre-graced condition.

TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor that closes and contains the entire sacramental system; the eschatological completion toward which all seven sacraments orient.

AFWD.justification: The directed morphism of grace moving from God through matter (water, oil, bread, wine) into the soul; ascent toward holiness.

AREV.justification: The reverse morphism; descent into acknowledgment of sin, dissolution of separation, return to grace through contrition and absolution.

CLINK.justification: Sequential composition: Baptism → Confirmation → Eucharist → Penance → Anointing → Holy Orders → Matrimony; each sacrament links to the next in the economy of grace.

IMSCRIB.justification: Identity and self-reference: 'This is my body, this is my blood' — the sacrament that recognizes itself as the presence of Christ; the stone that knows itself.

FSPLIT.justification: The first sacrament splits the soul into two branches: the baptized (T-arm: incorporated into Christ) and the unbaptized (F-arm: outside the covenant). Input: one soul; outputs: [member of the Church, non-member].

FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the baptized soul after sin has fractured it into [guilty/separated] and [grace-seeking]. FFUSE(FSPLIT(baptized soul)) = restored baptized soul in communion.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative evaluation: the sacrament is properly administered (correct words, correct substance, correct intention); grace is efficacious.

EVALF.justification: Negative evaluation: the sacrament is profaned, misdirected, or improperly performed; grace does not flow; the soul remains in its prior state.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: The mystery of the Incarnation in the Eucharist
ENGAGR.justification: A paradice held without resolution: bread AND body, wine AND blood, material AND divine, simultaneously present. Both states held in the albedo stage of transubstantiation.

IFIX.justification: Permanent, append-only, irreversible record: these three sacraments imprint an indelible mark on the soul that cannot be undone, even if the recipient falls into sin.

phase_2.split_input: One human soul seeking entry into the covenant
split_outputs: Baptized soul (incorporated into the Mystical Body, T-arm)
phase_2.fuse_result: Restored baptized soul in full communion with the Church

phase_3.void_description: 00 — The soul before any sacrament; uninitialized, pre-graced, outside the economy of salvation.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — The soul in a state of sanctifying grace; incorporated into Christ; capable of receiving further sacraments; moving toward holiness.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — The soul in mortal sin; separated from grace; in need of Penance; the sacramental channels are blocked until reconciliation.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — The Eucharistic paradice: the consecrated species simultaneously bread and body, wine and blood, material and divine, held in mystery without rational resolution.

steps.domain_action: The unbaptized soul emerges from the void — no grace, no incorporation, no sacramental capacity.
The intention to baptize moves forward; the priest approaches with water and the Trinitarian formula.
Baptism splits the soul into two branches: [baptized/incorporated] and [unbaptized/excluded].
The baptismal form and matter are valid; the words are spoken correctly; the water touches the forehead; grace flows.
The indelible character of Baptism is imprinted on the soul — permanent, irreversible, a mark that cannot be erased.
The baptized soul is now linked to the next sacrament in the chain.
Confirmation approaches; the bishop anoints with chrism; the Holy Spirit is invoked.
The chrism is genuine; the words are correct; the intention is pastoral; grace strengthens the soul.
The indelible character of Confirmation is imprinted — a second permanent mark, sealing the soul as a soldier of Christ.
The confirmed soul advances to the Eucharist.
The Eucharist is celebrated; 'This is my body, this is my blood' — the sacrament recognizes itself as the presence of Christ.
The consecrated bread and wine are simultaneously material and divine, held in the paradice of transubstantiation without rational resolution.
The Eucharistic form and matter are valid; the priest has the proper intention; the faithful receive the body and blood of Christ.
Grace flows through the Eucharist into the communicant; union with Christ is deepened; the soul is nourished.
The soul continues through life; sin may fracture it.
The soul falls into mortal sin; it descends into separation from grace; the Eucharistic channels are blocked.
The soul is now in a state of grave offense; it cannot receive Communion; it is excluded from the T-arm.
Penance begins; the soul is split between [guilt/separation] and [grace-seeking/contrition].
The penitent descends into acknowledgment of sin; contrition is expressed; the will turns back toward God.
The confession is sincere; the priest has jurisdiction; the absolution is pronounced with proper form.
Penance reconstitutes the baptized soul; the two branches [guilt] and [grace-seeking] are fused back into [restored communion].
Grace flows again; the soul is reconciled; the Eucharistic channels reopen.
The soul may now receive the Eucharist again; the chain continues.
As the soul matures, it may be called to Holy Orders or Matrimony.
Holy Orders splits the soul into [ordained/set apart for ministry] and [lay/not ordained].
The ordination is valid; the bishop has authority; the proper matter and form are used; the candidate is a baptized male.
The indelible character of Holy Orders is imprinted — a third permanent mark that configures the soul to Christ the Priest.
The ordained minister is now a conduit of grace; he administers the sacraments to others.
Alternatively, the soul may be called to Matrimony.
Matrimony splits the soul into [married/sacramentally united] and [unmarried/single].
The marriage is valid; both parties consent freely; they are baptized; the proper form is observed.
Grace flows into the married couple; they are sanctified as a domestic church; their union becomes a sign of Christ's love for the Church.
The married couple lives out their vocation; they may raise children; they receive the Eucharist together.
As the soul ages, it approaches the end of life.
The soul descends toward death; the body weakens; the final journey begins.
The Anointing of the Sick is administered; the priest anoints the forehead and hands with sacred oil.
The anointing is valid; the soul is in danger of death or grave illness; the proper form is used; grace is imparted for the final passage.
Grace strengthens the soul for the final trial; sins are forgiven; the soul is prepared for judgment.
The soul descends through death; the body returns to dust; the soul stands before God.
The Church Triumphant receives the soul; the sacramental economy is complete; the soul enters into the communion of saints.
The entire sacramental life is recognized as a unified whole — seven sacraments, one grace, one Christ, one Church.
The soul's journey through the seven sacraments is now a permanent record in the communion of saints.
The soul's sacramental history is fixed in eternity; it cannot be undone; it is a witness to the efficacy of grace.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The Holy Spirit, working through the Church's magisterium and the ordained priesthood, translates the intention to sanctify into sacramental operations; the Spirit ensures that matter, form, and intention align to produce grace.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: The sacraments communicate grace from God through the Church to the soul via the sensible signs (water, oil, bread, wine, words); each sacrament is a channel of communication between the divine and human orders.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The indelible character imprinted by Baptism, Confirmation, and Holy Orders serves as permanent memory; the soul's sacramental history is inscribed in the communion of saints; God's memory of the soul is infallible.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The liturgical calendar and the soul's life stages determine the order and timing of the sacraments; Baptism comes first, then Confirmation, then regular Eucharist and Penance, then Holy Orders or Matrimony, then Anointing of the Sick at the end.
phase_5.alfs_store: The Eucharist is the canonical reference and bootstrap store — the sacrament of sacraments, the source and summit of the Christian life, the perpetual presence of Christ that sustains the entire sacramental economy.

phase_6.cycle_cost: The soul's free will and cooperation; the priest's intention and valid administration; the Church's fidelity to the apostolic tradition; the matter and form prescribed by Christ.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: A soul in a state of nature, separated from God, subject to sin and death, without access to the supernatural order.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: A soul incorporated into Christ, sanctified by grace, capable of eternal communion with God, marked with indelible characters, and integrated into the Mystical Body of the Church.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The sacramental cycle is entropy-conservative because grace is not created or destroyed but transmitted; the soul's capacity for holiness is actualized without loss; the seven sacraments form a closed economy in which matter, form, and grace circulate eternally without dissipation. The Eucharist perpetually renews the cycle, maintaining the system in a state of dynamic equilibrium oriented toward the eschaton.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → IFIX → CLINK → AFWD → EVALT → IFIX → CLINK → IMSCRIB → ENGAGR → EVALT → AFWD → CLINK → AREV → EVALF → FSPLIT → AREV → EVALT → FFUSE → AFWD → CLINK → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → IFIX → AFWD → CLINK → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → CLINK → AFWD → AREV → AFWD → EVALT → AFWD → AREV → TANCH → IMSCRIB → CLINK → IFIX
-- Class: The 7 Sacraments
-- Fingerprint: sig=(25,5,9,4)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=3
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=43
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(17, 20)]

import Imscribing.IGFunctor

-- truth arm
noncomputable def the_7_sacraments_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (the_7_sacraments_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
